name: Nuosu

sources.author: Gerner, Matthias
sources.title: A Grammar of Nuosu
sources.notes: Reference grammar; the source for a future syllabary map.

notes: Nuosu / Sichuan Yi (ꆇꉙ) — Loloish (Ngwi) language of Sichuan, written in the Yi script. INPUT CONTRACT: the modern standard Yi script is a SYLLABARY of 819 standardised syllabograms (each glyph = one CV syllable with tone), so no letter-level grapheme map exists or is claimed here; THE GRAPHEME INVENTORY IS NOT YET SPECIFIED (graphemes/allophones are empty). A full syllabogram→IPA table, or else romanised input in the official Yi Pinyin (e.g. ⟨bbop⟩), would be required — exactly as `zh` requires pinyin. Nuosu is tonal (four tones, written by tone-diacritic-derived glyph variants).
